reviews.review_title: Genuinely makes me happy to play!
reviews.review_description: I'm really enjoying Retroneka. It's such a unique and fun game which brings me happiness. I play it frequently from the comfort of my bed with no motion sickness, which makes it a perfect way for me to unwind. I feel It's a well made game and is a great value for the price.
Great game. Definitely worth the money.

This game is extremely well-made and lots of fun! It requires some real hand-eye coordination and multitasking, which can occasionally be difficult, but that's ok, it's worth the challenge. The driving is smooth, the shooting is accurate and clean, and the missions are cool. The graphics make me feel like a delivery driver in Futurama, hyper stylish stuff. GREAT WORK DEVS!

My only complaint is that the button that "speeds up" the dialogue, like you press B once and the scrolling text instantly populates, does NOT do that for the last line of dialogue in any given conversation. Instead, it skips that dialogue completely, often causing me to miss what would have been said. Hopefully it's an easy fix but it's not bad enough to lower my rating from 5 stars.

The easy difficulty was just challenging enough for me to finish the game.  I don't see how it is possible to play this without changing the command to joystick.

Drive with one hand and shoot with the other.    If you love this game like i do, try GAZZLER.  Good job Devs !

Retronika is a very satisfying game to play. Cel-shaded graphics are simple but nice to look at, and all the moving objects around you create strong immersion. The gameplay is smooth and I love the upgrade system.

However, not being able to utilize vertical movement while holding the gun might be a game-breaker for me. I'm not sure if this was a conscious choice by the devs, but it doesn't really add any value to the game, other than throwing in unrealistic difficulty. Couldn't difficulty be increased in another way that doesn't hinder gameplay?

Also, how in the world do I activate the shields that I purchased? I can see it in the middle of my bike's handlebar, but none of the buttons on my controllers activate them. As is, they're useless.

Please consider allowing vertical movement to be controlled with one hand on the handlebars, and not just two. And please include some information somewhere for how to use the shields!

Very Fun. 
Very Intuitive.
Different.
Stylish.
Cool.

For who thinks that it is too difficult: Learn how to Ride with one hand and shoot with the other one and you will end the game. Just sometimes stop and shoot both hands to clean up the drones.

SUPERB FOR THE PRICE!

reviews.review_title: Retronika is so much fun! Buy it now!!!
reviews.review_description: I absolutely love Retronika! An awesome game that's not only super cheap, but takes up very little space on my headset. I couldn't be more pleased with the purchase. So much fun!!! Zero motion sickness, which is crazy to me. All you have to do is watch the trailer to know that this game is pretty darn awesome. It lives up to the trailer & so much more. All the 5-star reviews know what they're talking about & can express it better than me. All I know is I have hundreds of apps & Retronika is one of the best I've come across when it comes to innovation, uniqueness, creativity, user-friendly menu, intuitive controls, gameplay & a touch of silliness. A top-tier game for sure - up there with Pistol Whip, Smash Drums, Mini Golf, etc. I am so grateful this game found its way to me. If you love VR & love to have fun, purchase this game!

developer_response.body: Thank you Mike, we really appreciate your honest & detailed review! ❤️

reviews.review_description: Quirky, fun, fast-paced and action-packed! The art style is really cool and unique. I just wish you could preview cosmetic upgrades before you spend precious (in-game) credits on them. Looking forward to more!

TL;DR So far the game is fun, challenging, and very polished (aside from a few minor typos I noticed), and already worth it IMO, especially at the €9/$9 sale price (40% off, normally 15). You're definitely getting good bang for your buck.


I just played it for about 2 hours (until the Q3 battery died, which should tell you something).

The gameplay kinda reminds me of Runner, except you're navigating up and down as well. Each level is a straight 'road', though the challenge comes from navigating through the very dense and unpredictable traffic, while often having to fight multiple different types of enemies.
I played on the second-easiest difficulty and still had to restart a few levels at this early stage.
You can steer the bike either with motion controls, or with the thumbstick. 

There's a healthy variety of weapons, though I've only unlocked 5 of them so far (I've seen a good few more in the trailers). Each weapon has a different look as well. You can also wield two of the same, or two different weapons simultaneously.

Each level is short, about 2-3 minutes, so perfect for bite-sized gaming sessions.

It has a gorgeous, clean cel-shaded art style. I got to level 25, and encountered maybe 6 different types of enemies (including a boss), all with different looks and attack styles.

There seems to be a good variety of music, though I haven't paid too much attention to it yet.

I don't know how long the campaign is, but going by my progress, I'm guessing 5+ hours. There's also an endless mode that I've yet to try.

My one gripe is that I would've liked to see the path be more than a straight line, though maybe that'd make things too difficult. Maybe in the endless mode with no enemies. Or maybe things change in the later levels?

Regardless, the game is a joy to play and stands out from the crowd.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review & feedback, we really appreciate it! After a busy launch week, we are already working on hotfixes and feedback from the community. It's just the beginning for us!

reviews.review_title: Great Look & Fun, but Too Challenging..
reviews.review_description:
I love the look of the game, the feel of it, and it's fun up until the first boss. I've been playing on the easiest difficulty since I don't have the vision needed to keep up with traffic, the drones that fly in shooting all around you, and everything on the radar which looking at means I'm taking my one eye off of everything else and generally taking damage on a toughdr level like the impossible FIRST boss...

So now the game is useless to me as I can't upgrade to soften the difficulty nor can make it any "easier" thsn it already is.. I'm hitting the unpassable difficulty wall on Easy and STILL have 40-50 more (even tougher I'm sure) levels to go if I wss to ever get lucky (luck is all it would be...) and beat this bose...

So don't buy the game if you are not up for crazy difficult games or just can't handle them, as otherwise this one will lose it's charm for you very quickly.

developer_response.body:
Hey Teele! With the release of the game, we actually adjusted all of the difficulties to a more enjoyable level. We heard this feedback prior to release so we wanted to make so changes, right now it should definitely feel more enjoyable than it was before!

However in your case, we would advise you to get acquainted with the controls of the game - it might seem hard at first but it's easy to master! 😅Thanks for the review, we'll keep an eye on the difficulty levels!

reviews.review_description:
Seriously Retronika is like a mashup of so many game genres. It really gives me Futurama design vibes. On-rails shooters (with X/Y axis freedom) meets Borderlands Cel shaded art style, meets almost Beat Saber-like game flow. Running the campaign is a blast and I love all the little details I see even making runs ‘one more time’. The music is great and fits the mid-century retro-future focus - wish there was more though! Can’t wait to see where it develops into. Maybe multiplayer or multi-path levels? 

Do not sleep on this game

This game is great. It has the magic that beat saber has where you don't actually move your perspective much which is great for those who experience motion sickness in movement heavy games. 

An open-city RPG in Retronika universe with quests that is 6-10 hours long with radiant/replayable quests gets an instant buy from me @ $40!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! As a fellow motion sickness enjoyer, I can say that I am also really happy to see players with motion sickness enjoying Retronika!

Hope to see you in Retronika again. 🏍️

reviews.review_description: Wasnt expecting much out of this tbh but after it came on Spring sale I purchased and this guys is FUN! easy rider style shooter. I play sat on a small furniture bench between my legs and feels like I'm actually on this bike. Love the cartoon style graphics and it's a neat little game. Smooth controls which surprised me, ZERO motion sickness which again surprised me. Well polished graphics too Enough upgrades to keep me busy and cool customisation of bike. Really like this this fun shooter something a bit different. Sometimes simple is better and in this case its better. B+ defo worth a purchase even at full price.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words and detailed review, we really appreciate it! ❤️

reviews.review_description:
This game completely caught me by surprise because I didn't think I would like it as much as I have. The mechanics are fun and easy to learn. There are a plethora of upgrades for your speeder bike and about 7 or 8 different weapons you can use. There are several different types of enemies you will encounter to keep you on your toes and a few different boss fights to keep things interesting.

This game is pretty epic because while you're in the middle of shooting down drones and dodging attacks, you are also weaving in, out, and around traffic. It's adds for some very enjoyable chaos, but also makes for a challenging game.

Seeing the NinjaGuyVR keychain was a nice surprise too! I recommend this game to anyone looking for some challenging fun.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, this is just the beginning for us as well! 👀

If you haven't tried yet, try Endless Mode and challenge yourself to other players. 😅

reviews.review_description: I think this game has a lot of great ideas, decent art direction, controls, unique gameplay and good gameplay progression. The thing that annoys me is this is sooo close to being an instant recommendation. The up down left and right flying works great, the shooting works great, the enemy types are varied, so are the weapons. However, there are some minor tweaks to the overall game that would make this incredible. First of all, there are no turns, or hills, just flying in a laser blocked tunnel or actual tunnels. Adding some parts where you climb, dive or take a turn would add so much more immersion. The flying controls great, but there is no tilt or pitch to the bike, it just slides. It has acceleration to those controls, but if you were flying like this, the bike would tilt. Sure this could cause more motion sickness for some, but you could turn it off. I also wish there were fewer cars. I know the chaos of it adds the challenge, but the randomness of the moving vehicles can make some runs miserable. I love the sheer number of cars on screen, but when racing the clock, being shot at, and losing vertical control when firing it is frustrating having a truck fly up under you and shove you out of the top of the track and force you to holster your weapon and drop back to the track and start again. It breaks really cool moments sometimes. There are a bunch of tiny tweaks, but this is long enough. TLDR; fun game, could be a great game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback SpiritDonkey! We really appreciate it and will make sure that our team will have a look. 🫡

We are, however, really happy to see that you had fun!

reviews.review_description:
Retronika is a simple to understand game that takes skill to master. 

This is an arcade-styled shooter where you and your trusty bike must navigate the skies with precision while surviving the hostility of your enemies - coming in the form of drones and other flying vehicles. 

Think of this game as being inspired by something like subway surfers, but mixed with almost a roguelite element. 

Flying the bike around is fun and intuitive, with the ability to navigate horizontally one handed, but requiring two to go vertical. This means you can't always have one hand on a gun - and making it to the end of a level becomes a little more challenging. 

There's a lot of customization to be done here on the bike and weapons, all these things can be done/ unlocked by playing each level and getting three stars. Hopefully the roadmap the developer has in mind includes more bike types that provide different perks as this would make replaying much more interesting. 


Overall fun game for those who want a sitting experience

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and the review! We really appreciate your interest and there is more cooking in the oven indeed. 👀 But first, our priority stands with the feedback and hotfixes!

Hope to see you again in Retronika.

reviews.review_title: Great title, especially for the price
reviews.review_description:
If you have played the game "runner" on here (great game) and enjoyed it, then this is a variation of that. 

It adds a bit more to the mix with weapon and bike modifications. Also, this game has verticality which adds a lot to the mix. 

I would highly recommend this.

I found the graphics style to be fun and immersive, and the game is easy to get into and progresses well 

The 3d/vr motion is handled well, I don't get sick from playing it, and enjoy being in this imaginary world.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review!

reviews.review_description:
Definitely a top VR fav now. 
Im a fan of multitasking shooters 
Here you multitask driving a flying motorcycle thru traffic with roughly a dozen types of guns and over 40 levels. Tons of replay ability. Especially with endless mode! It gets chaotic and super fast and tons of enemies on the screen.
Challenging but not impossible to master :) 
The graphics look great for the comic style. Great controls, you drive up and down and left and right to dodge, and bike can be upgraded with speed and handling. 
Lastly epic bosses
Awesome job devs

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, really appreciate your kind words!

Enjoy!

reviews.review_description:
It's cheap, has great presentation, lots of levels and upgrades, and has an addicting and well-executed challenge of driving while shooting in every direction! And unlike Attack on Titan: Unbreakable, these devs actually have been updating this game with new modes and missions for free during this early access build! 
My only complaints so far is that there's not much story so far, aside from the dialogue from your bot. It would be nice for the final version to have more story context, like an opening cutscene and landmarks in the levels to represent your destinations. That, and I would like for my trigger to be the acceleration when it grips the handle for full immersion. It's hard to adjust to steering physically when my analog stick controls the gas. 
Other than that, not much else. Can't wait to see what's next!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, we really appreciate the kind words and the suggestions. ❤️

2025 is going to be even more exciting for us - hope you'll be around to see it! 😅

reviews.review_title: Alot of fun, try the infinity run mode!
reviews.review_description: The art style coupled with the racing or driving while taking down drones is alot of fun.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, we're really happy that you had fun!

reviews.review_title: Love it and I wasn't sick
reviews.review_description:
This is great. I took a gamble and had the sick bucket at the ready, but had no issues at all with the motion. Great controls and I like how the game eases you in. Robot guy calling me sweetheart was an added bonus. 

Can see me enjoying this for a while. 

Please make it multiplayer.
Great effort with wonderful cell shaded graphics and engrossing gameplay

developer_response.body: Thank you, we are glad that you had fun!

reviews.review_description: You blast dudes and fly around in a motorcycle what more can you ask for.

I have been eyeing this game for a bit. Decided to make the purchase and I'm glad I did! I'm still very early on in the game, but thus far it is awesome! I've always loved the thought of flying a car in the sky ever since I saw Back to the future. Now, I get to do just that but on a motorcycle! The feel and rush you get from this is truly remarkable. The handling feels good, mechanics all work well. The amount of traffic you must manover around constantly keeps you on high alert. The art style and scenery blends in very well. Many upgrades are available which is also great. I'm still early on in the campaign and can't wait to dive back into it. This was well worth the price and I'd strongly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review and kind words! More updates are coming soon, hope you'll like them as well! 😅

reviews.review_description:
I'm really grateful to have participated in the testing process for Retronika and can only echo the other positive reviews given here. 

The flying is exhilarating and the mechanics have been implemented perfectly. The cell shaded graphics are beautiful and immersive. The gunplay is smooth and balances well with the steering. Gameplay is fun, challenging and rewarding. All of these elements are further enhanced as you unlock bike and weapon upgrades along the way.

Although the world-building aspect is perhaps superfluous for a game of this type, the more I play the more invested I've become in seeing how the story develops in later levels.

I definitely think it's worth purchasing even in early access as it already offers a ton of content (50 levels in total) with lots of variety and replayability. The currently listed UK/EU meta store price is an absolute bargain- I would have paid that for the alpha build, which only had 5 levels!

I believe I'm correct in stating that difficulty settings plus an 'endless mode' will be added by the devs at some point in the future. The former should greatly improve accessibility so that all levels of gamer get to fully experience what Retronika has to offer. The latter will make a nice alternative to the campaign mode, for those just looking for the 'thrill of the flight'.

Enjoy!
Without VR, I never would have known the thrill of flying a motorcycle through a traffic jam in a vibrant floating city while shooting down killer robots.  See you on the leaderboards!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the awesome review! We’re thrilled to hear you enjoyed the adrenaline of zooming through traffic and battling robots in Retronika! 🚀 We’re looking forward to seeing you climb those leaderboards. Keep up the great work!

reviews.review_description: Enjoying my time commuting in this beautiful game! I am playing more for the driving than shooting although both are fun. Interested to see what else will be added, and if the highways get any crazier as I progress

developer_response.body: Thank you cozel! We are already working on the next update and can't wait to share it with you all on the 12th! 👀

reviews.review_description:
Love the aesthetic, the gameplay, the music is super on point, everything. This game is deserving of a 5 star rating and makes me very happy. :) 

The game also has difficulty options so that yiu can adjust how easy or how challenging you want it to be.

The game also now has bosses.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! We are already working on new updates and some cool features, hope to see you again! 👀

reviews.review_description: Ok, so you don't exactly get to be Mila Jovovich and escape a tall building and fall down into a taxi cab and then evade police. BUT you do get to drive around in a similar (albeit cartoonier) looking world, shooting down drones and turrets and bosses, running through red lights, and dodging (or trying to dodge) reckless drivers who never signal. I do think it would be cool if some of the drivers did indicate before moving (especially the ones that do appear to have lights on the back of their vehicles), but that's just a nitpicky thing I'd like to see. Also bhaptic support would be amazing 🤞 . Great game, highly recommend!

Honestly I can't describe how much fun it is to drive this bike through the city and fight against enemies.

And I love the art style. It simply looks incredible.

developer_response.body: We're really happy to hear you're enjoying Retronika! Thanks for sharing your excitement — it means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description: 3D comic style cyberpunk motosports, what a mix! You race the motor into all directions by stretching your body, kind of physical exercise, my knees sour after half an hour play. Tons of missions, upgrade your motor and equip yourself after each session, quite fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review! Your feedback keeps us motivated to keep creating even more for you 💙💙💙

reviews.review_description: Really cool, so cool

developer_response.body: Thanks for the recommendation! Glad you’re enjoying it!🤘

reviews.review_title: The Most fun I've had in a VR Game
reviews.review_description: I fell in love with this game since I watched the very first video. It kind of felt like I was going to be transported into Futurama and I was not wrong at all! With amazing art style, a lot of action and lots of challenges, I can't stop playing this game and if you like to have a feel of riding a retro-futuristic motorcycle while fighting against traffic and robots, you must give this a try!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the amazing feedback! We’re pumped that you’re loving the retro-futuristic vibes and nonstop action! 🔥 It’s awesome to hear the game has you hooked. Keep riding, keep battling, and we’ll see you on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_description: This game has it all.  Relaxing drive runs, survival runs,  blast enemies and drive with one hand.  The mechanics and controls of this game is top notch.  These developers nailed the feeling of riding a futuristic motorcycle.   Add Boss battles and leaderboards and you have many reasons to play this game over and over.  Highly recommended!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review! Players like you, reaching the top and enjoying every moment, inspire us to keep creating more and more. 🚀
